content:
type: QUESTION
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: I'm adding get_image_mimetype() method (lazy mimetype resolution+caching) and updating image_to_base64 to call it instead of inline filetype.guess logic. Both touch the image_to_base64 validator - can you share the exact shape of your normalize_image_bytes helper so I can adapt, or should I go first since my change is small?

fields.summary: I'm adding get_image_mimetype() method (lazy mimetype resolution+caching) and updating image_to_base64 to call it instead of inline filetype.guess logic. Both touch the image_to_base64 validator - can you share the exact shape of your normalize_image_bytes helper so I can adapt, or should I go first since my change is small?

content:
type: STATUS
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: My repo copy doesn't show your normalize_image_bytes change (likely isolated environments) - I implemented get_image_mimetype() on top of the original inline-guess code: it checks image_mimetype cache, guesses via filetype.guess(self.image) if unset, caches, and returns it; image_to_base64 now calls self.get_image_mimetype() before b64-encoding. My patch.txt only touches this method pair so it should merge cleanly with your normalize_image_bytes extraction if applied on top - just have image_to_base64 call get_image_mimetype() on decoded_for_guessing instead of self.image before encoding. Done on my end.
